  "findings": [
    "Loud, persistent 120 Hz electrical hum/buzzing indicating a loose connection, overloaded circuit, or failing breaker",
    "Potential thermal hazard and fire risk due to poor contact resistance at terminations"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the panel immediately to isolate the circuit",
    "Perform a thermal imaging scan and torque test on all terminations",
    "Inspect breaker contacts and busbar for signs of pitting, arcing, or thermal damage",
    "Replace any damaged breakers or degraded conductors"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals a severe, continuous 120 Hz electrical buzz characteristic of a loose termination or failing contact under load. This creates a high risk of localized overheating, arcing, and electrical fire. Immediate isolation and corrective maintenance are required under NEC 110.14.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14 (Electrical Connections)",
    "NEC 110.12 (Mechanical Execution of Work)",
    "NFPA 70E (Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace)"
  ]
}
